title.en: Shabbat Games and Activities: Fun Without Screens
title.zh: 安息日游戏与活动：没有屏幕的快乐
title.he: משחקים ופעילויות בשבת: כיף ללא מסכים

author: Torah Light Editorial Team

summary.en: A comprehensive guide to screen-free Shabbat entertainment for families with children of all ages, including traditional games, creative activities, and cultural parallels to Chinese family traditions.
summary.zh: 适合所有年龄段儿童的家庭的全面无屏幕安息日娱乐指南，包括传统游戏、创意活动以及与中国家庭传统的文化对比。
summary.he: מדריך מקיף לבידור שבת ללא מסכים למשפחות עם ילדים בכל הגילאים, כולל משחקים מסורתיים, פעילויות יצירתיות ומקבילות תרבותיות למסורות משפחתיות סיניות.

bibliography.title: The Sabbath: Its Meaning for Modern Man
bibliography.author: Abraham Joshua Heschel

targetAudience: Mandarin-speaking families exploring Orthodox Judaism, parents seeking screen-free Shabbat activities, educators working with Chinese Jewish communities
culturalContext: Includes parallels to traditional Chinese games (Go, Chinese chess) and family gathering customs during Chinese festivals, emphasizing shared values of multi-generational connection and unplugged family time.
halachicNote: This article reflects mainstream Orthodox practice. For specific halachic questions regarding muktzeh, games with dice, or permissible Shabbat activities, readers should consult their local rabbi.
